Art title 1: My Heart Is Yours But It Still Hurts Sometimes (weeping was heard)
Artist Statement 1:
Just because we are with God, doesn't mean we will have an easy, happy life. In fact, besides being told to live "quiet lives" in 1 Thessalonians, the Bible mostly describes Christians dealing with hardships.

But what we do know is that God is with us in it. In fact in my experience, it is in hardship that I have recognized God closest. It is okay to come to God when you feel pain. And though he may not make it go away, it makes the pain sweeter to be held by someone who understands pain.
excerpt:
Sometimes I'm afraid I'm asking God for too much stuff, like he can only afford to do one or two things for me each day.

But I don't really think He has a certain number of dollars in his billfold, or only 24 hours in his days.

I think it's more like a valve in a levy. The more we ask of him, as long as it's the stuff he knows is really good for us, the more he is willing to flood our kingdom with His. I think the limiting factor is us; how much good we are able to take.

Raw Spoon
3dNotes3: Directly below this is the Mount of Olives
